computedFrom: Date the money was lent or contract performance fell due
notes: 3-year period under Schedule I, Article 18 (money payable for money lent).

computedFrom: Date the money was paid for the plaintiff
notes: If the contract fixes a different due date, anchor on that. 3 years from accrual of cause of action.

matterType: Price of goods sold and delivered
computedFrom: Date of delivery of goods
Date of dishonour / date instrument became payable
notes: Civil recovery suit on the instrument. Distinct from §138 NI Act criminal complaint window.

computedFrom: Date fixed for performance, or date plaintiff had notice of refusal
notes: If the contract fixes a date, run from that. Else from the date the plaintiff had notice that performance was refused.

computedFrom: Date the contract was broken
Date the tort was committed
notes: 1-year residual tort period. Specific torts (defamation, malicious prosecution) have their own articles.

computedFrom: Date of the injury
notes: Motor accidents are governed by §166 of the Motor Vehicles Act 1988 (no outer bar after 2019 amendment).

computedFrom: Date the prosecution terminated in plaintiff's favour
Date the libel was published / date the plaintiff knew of slander

matterType: Possession of immovable property based on title
computedFrom: Date possession of the defendant became adverse
notes: 12-year clock starts only when the defendant's possession turns adverse — not from initial entry.

computedFrom: Date of dispossession
notes: Suit for possession on the strength of prior possession; 12 years.

computedFrom: Date the right to redeem accrued
notes: 30 years.

computedFrom: Date the right to sue first accrued
Date of exclusion from joint possession
notes: 12 years from when the plaintiff's possession of joint property is ousted.

computedFrom: Date the right to sue accrues
notes: 3 years; the residual article for suits not otherwise provided for.

computedFrom: Date the right to apply accrued
notes: 3 years; the residual article for applications not otherwise provided for.

computedFrom: Date of the decree appealed from
notes: 90 days. Time for obtaining the certified copy is excluded under §12.

matterType: First appeal to subordinate civil court
computedFrom: Date of the decree appealed from
notes: 30 days.

computedFrom: Date of the decree or order
notes: 30 days.

computedFrom: Date of the decree or order sought to be revised
notes: 90 days.

computedFrom: Date of the cheque-dishonour memo
notes: Compound ladder: 30 days to issue notice + 15-day drawer window + 30 days to file complaint after window expires. Use the calculator ladder for precise milestones.

matterType: Application to set aside arbitral award (§34 A&C Act)
computedFrom: Date the party received the signed award
notes: Court may extend by another 30 days on sufficient cause; absolute bar after 3 months + 30 days.

matterType: Application for appointment of arbitrator (§11 A&C Act)
computedFrom: Date the right to apply accrued (typically the date the opposite party failed to appoint an arbitrator within 30 days of notice)
notes: 3 years under Article 137. See Bharat Sanchar Nigam Ltd v. Nortel Networks (2021) for accrual analysis.

computedFrom: Date on which the cause of action arose
notes: 2 years. Commission may admit beyond limitation on sufficient cause being shown.

computedFrom: Date of the impugned action
notes: No statutory limitation. Courts adopt 3 years by analogy to Article 137 of the Limitation Act and apply doctrine of delay & laches.

computedFrom: Date the order was made / communicated, or representation rejected
notes: 1 year. Service rules and CAT-jurisdiction filters apply separately.

computedFrom: Date of service of the assessment order or notice of demand
notes: 30 days. Form 35 e-filing required. CIT(A) may condone delay on sufficient cause.

computedFrom: Date of communication of the order appealed against
notes: 60 days. ITAT may admit a delayed appeal on sufficient cause.

computedFrom: Date on which the decision/order was communicated to the appellant
notes: 3 months for assessee appeals; appellate authority may condone a further 1 month.

computedFrom: Date of the impugned judgment / order of the High Court
notes: 90 days. Time for obtaining a certified copy is excluded.

matterType: Restoration of suit / setting aside ex parte decree
computedFrom: Date of the dismissal-for-default order / date of knowledge of the ex parte decree
notes: 30 days. §123 carries a separate accrual rule for ex parte decrees.
